<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Raijin-yama Kofun]][[en:Raijinyama Kofun]][[ja:雷神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660187]] Wikidata name: Raijinyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Natori, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Raijinyama Kofun | native_name = 雷神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Aerial photo of Raijin-yama kofun tumulus in 1975.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Raijinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Raijinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.151167|N|140.879667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Natori|WD=Q642018}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = 168 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1976-1977 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Raijinyama Kofun'''|雷神山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located approximately two kilometers south of the center of the city of [[:en:Natori, Miyagi|Natori, Miyagi]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site is a double mound, consisting of a large keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' with a smaller adjacent circular ''kofun''. Both were collectively designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1956, with the area covered by the designation expanded in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171395 |title=雷神山古墳 |trans-title=Raijinyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Raijinyama kofun is the largest {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}} in the Tōhoku region and is located at the top of Natori hill where many large tumuli are concentrated, overlooking the Sendai Plain. It has a total length of 168 meters, and was once partially surrounded by a moat. It was built in steps, partially by utilizing the contours of a natural hill, and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. A portion of the posterior tumulus collapsed during the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake]], suggesting that it contained a clay coffin or wooden coffin in a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].
North of the main ''kofun'' is a smaller domed tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters called the "Kozuka kofun".
The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It has only been partially excavated; however, From the style of the mound and the pot-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery fragments discovered at the site, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 4th or early 5th century AD.
At present, the area is being maintained as a historic park. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Tatekoshi Station|Tatekoshi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]].
Raijinyama kofun *Total length: 168 meters *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 96 meter diameter x 12 meter height *Keyway Portion: 72 meter length x 96 meter max width, 6 meter height, 2-stage
Kozuka kofun *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 54 meter diameter
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Tōmizuka Kofun]] *[[Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]]

color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Raijinyama Kofun'''|雷神山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located approximately two kilometers south of the center of the city of [[:en:Natori, Miyagi|Natori, Miyagi]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site is a double mound, consisting of a large keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' with a smaller adjacent circular ''kofun''. Both were collectively designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1956, with the area covered by the designation expanded in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171395 |title=雷神山古墳 |trans-title=Raijinyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Raijinyama kofun is the largest {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}} in the Tōhoku region and is located at the top of Natori hill where many large tumuli are concentrated, overlooking the Sendai Plain. It has a total length of 168 meters, and was once partially surrounded by a moat. It was built in steps, partially by utilizing the contours of a natural hill, and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. A portion of the posterior tumulus collapsed during the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake]], suggesting that it contained a clay coffin or wooden coffin in a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].
North of the main ''kofun'' is a smaller domed tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters called the "Kozuka kofun".
The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It has only been partially excavated; however, From the style of the mound and the pot-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery fragments discovered at the site, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 4th or early 5th century AD.
At present, the area is being maintained as a historic park. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Tatekoshi Station|Tatekoshi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]].
Raijinyama kofun *Total length: 168 meters *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 96 meter diameter x 12 meter height *Keyway Portion: 72 meter length x 96 meter max width, 6 meter height, 2-stage
Kozuka kofun *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 54 meter diameter

[[kofun]] | part_of = | length = 168 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1976-1977 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Raijinyama Kofun'''|雷神山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located approximately two kilometers south of the center of the city of [[:en:Natori, Miyagi|Natori, Miyagi]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site is a double mound, consisting of a large keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' with a smaller adjacent circular ''kofun''. Both were collectively designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1956, with the area covered by the designation expanded in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171395 |title=雷神山古墳 |trans-title=Raijinyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Raijinyama kofun is the largest {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}} in the Tōhoku region and is located at the top of Natori hill where many large tumuli are concentrated, overlooking the Sendai Plain. It has a total length of 168 meters, and was once partially surrounded by a moat. It was built in steps, partially by utilizing the contours of a natural hill, and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. A portion of the posterior tumulus collapsed during the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake]], suggesting that it contained a clay coffin or wooden coffin in a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].
North of the main ''kofun'' is a smaller domed tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters called the "Kozuka kofun".
The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It has only been partially excavated; however, From the style of the mound and the pot-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery fragments discovered at the site, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 4th or early 5th century AD.
At present, the area is being maintained as a historic park. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Tatekoshi Station|Tatekoshi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]].
Raijinyama kofun *Total length: 168 meters *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 96 meter diameter x 12 meter height *Keyway Portion: 72 meter length x 96 meter max width, 6 meter height, 2-stage
Kozuka kofun *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 54 meter diameter

Raijinyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Natori, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{Coord|38.151167|N|140.879667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Natori|WD=Q642018}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = 168 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1976-1977 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Raijinyama Kofun'''|雷神山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located approximately two kilometers south of the center of the city of {{ill|Natori, Miyagi|en|Natori, Miyagi|WD=Q642018|lt=Natori, Miyagi}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site is a double mound, consisting of a large keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' with a smaller adjacent circular ''kofun''. Both were collectively designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1956, with the area covered by the designation expanded in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171395 |title=雷神山古墳 |trans-title=Raijinyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Raijinyama kofun is the largest {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}} in the Tōhoku region and is located at the top of Natori hill where many large tumuli are concentrated, overlooking the Sendai Plain. It has a total length of 168 meters, and was once partially surrounded by a moat. It was built in steps, partially by utilizing the contours of a natural hill, and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. A portion of the posterior tumulus collapsed during the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake}}, suggesting that it contained a clay coffin or wooden coffin in a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].
North of the main ''kofun'' is a smaller domed tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters called the "Kozuka kofun".
The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It has only been partially excavated; however, From the style of the mound and the pot-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery fragments discovered at the site, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 4th or early 5th century AD.
At present, the area is being maintained as a historic park. It is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Tatekoshi Station|en|Tatekoshi Station|WD=Q899090|lt=Tatekoshi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}}.
Raijinyama kofun *Total length: 168 meters *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 96 meter diameter x 12 meter height *Keyway Portion: 72 meter length x 96 meter max width, 6 meter height, 2-stage
Kozuka kofun *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 54 meter diameter
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}} *[[Tōmizuka Kofun]] *[[Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Raijin-yama Kofun]][[en:Raijinyama Kofun]][[ja:雷神山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Raijin-yama Kofun]][[en:Raijinyama Kofun]][[ja:雷神山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Raijin-yama Kofun]][[en:Raijinyama Kofun]][[ja:雷神山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Natori, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Raijinyama Kofun | native_name = 雷神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Aerial photo of Raijin-yama kofun tumulus in 1975.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Raijinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Raijinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.151167|N|140.879667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Natori|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = 168 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1976-1977 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Raijinyama Kofun'''|雷神山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located approximately two kilometers south of the center of the city of {{ill|Natori, Miyagi|en|Natori, Miyagi|WD=Q642018|lt=Natori, Miyagi|1=Natori, Miyagi}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site is a double mound, consisting of a large keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' with a smaller adjacent circular ''kofun''. Both were collectively designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1956, with the area covered by the designation expanded in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171395 |title=雷神山古墳 |trans-title=Raijinyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Raijinyama kofun is the largest {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}} in the Tōhoku region and is located at the top of Natori hill where many large tumuli are concentrated, overlooking the Sendai Plain. It has a total length of 168 meters, and was once partially surrounded by a moat. It was built in steps, partially by utilizing the contours of a natural hill, and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. A portion of the posterior tumulus collapsed during the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, suggesting that it contained a clay coffin or wooden coffin in a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].
North of the main ''kofun'' is a smaller domed tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters called the "Kozuka kofun".
The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It has only been partially excavated; however, From the style of the mound and the pot-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery fragments discovered at the site, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 4th or early 5th century AD.
At present, the area is being maintained as a historic park. It is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Tatekoshi Station|en|Tatekoshi Station|WD=Q899090|lt=Tatekoshi Station|1=Tatekoshi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line|1=Tōhoku Main Line}}.
Raijinyama kofun *Total length: 168 meters *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 96 meter diameter x 12 meter height *Keyway Portion: 72 meter length x 96 meter max width, 6 meter height, 2-stage
Kozuka kofun *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 54 meter diameter
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}} *[[Tōmizuka Kofun]] *[[Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]]

==Overview== The Raijinyama kofun is the largest {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}} in the Tōhoku region and is located at the top of Natori hill where many large tumuli are concentrated, overlooking the Sendai Plain. It has a total length of 168 meters, and was once partially surrounded by a moat. It was built in steps, partially by utilizing the contours of a natural hill, and was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. A portion of the posterior tumulus collapsed during the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, suggesting that it contained a clay coffin or wooden coffin in a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].
North of the main ''kofun'' is a smaller domed tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters called the "Kozuka kofun".
The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It has only been partially excavated; however, From the style of the mound and the pot-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery fragments discovered at the site, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 4th or early 5th century AD.
At present, the area is being maintained as a historic park. It is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Tatekoshi Station|en|Tatekoshi Station|WD=Q899090|lt=Tatekoshi Station|1=Tatekoshi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line|1=Tōhoku Main Line}}.
Raijinyama kofun *Total length: 168 meters *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 96 meter diameter x 12 meter height *Keyway Portion: 72 meter length x 96 meter max width, 6 meter height, 2-stage
Kozuka kofun *Circular Portion: 3-stages, 54 meter diameter
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}} *[[Tōmizuka Kofun]] *[[Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]]
